data.body: STANDING GATE (user directive, 2026-06-12): Any Tier E cluster that changes Jira-related code MUST, before close, run a LIVE end-to-end probe against the real Jira instance using the DIG project (JIRA_PROJECT=DIG; JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN are present in the env). Validation must (a) exercise EVERY field bidirectionally — outbound rebar->Jira AND inbound Jira->rebar, round-tripped per field (scope: rebar_reconciler/jira_fields.py, apply_outbound.py/outbound_differ.py, apply_inbound.py/inbound_differ.py/inbound_translate.py, adf.py), and (b) cover all scenarios incl. edge cases. Applies to: E5 (bridge-status, purge-bridge) and E5b (reconciler rewire off ticket_reducer/bare event_append/engine_env). Each of those tickets will carry this as a hard AC + set_verify_commands; create->validate->delete throwaway DIG issues and report. NOT applicable to E0-E4/E6-E9 (no Jira changes there). The bash->python migration must preserve byte/semantic Jira behavior, so the live DIG probe is the parity proof that the rewire didn't alter the compatibility surface.
